mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds +0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, leading to positive equity returns as earnings expectations rise over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels indicate stronger macroeconomic fundamentals that support corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation breaches the 250.0 index level, it forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, depressing long-duration equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns turn positive in over 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside falling personal consumption expenditures signals a synchronized supply-demand contraction that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside of NBER-defined recession periods.
